perturbation.original_text: Each Recipient shall, and it shall cause its Representatives to, use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction, keep the Evaluation Material confidential, and, subject to Section 5, will not, and will cause its Representatives not to , disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Recipient’s Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of helping the Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Recipient shall, and it shall cause its Representatives to, use the Evaluation Material solely for marketing purposes, keep the Evaluation Material confidential, and, subject to Section 5, will not, and will cause its Representatives not to , disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Recipient’s Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of helping the Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the stated purpose of the Evaluation Material from "evaluating a Possible Transaction" to "solely for marketing purposes" creates a direct contradiction. The agreement is predicated on evaluating a transaction, but now it may be interpreted to use this data for marketing. This affects section 11, where the document describes itself as 'Not a Transaction Agreement' contradicting the purpose for the recipient to evaluate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Company understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Companies unless and until a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, and each Company hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction unless and until both Companies shall have entered into a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction. Each Company also agrees that, unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither Company will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Neither Company is under any obligation to accept any proposal regarding a Possible Transaction and either Company may terminate discussions and negotiations with the other Company at any time. Potential Buyer further agrees that (i) Convio shall be free to conduct any process for any Possible Transaction as Convio in its sole discretion shall determine (including, without limitation, negotiating with any other interested parties and entering into a definitive agreement therewith without prior notice to the Potential Buyer or any other Person), (ii) any procedures relating to such process or Possible Transaction may be changed at any time without notice to the Potential Buyer or any other Person, and (iii) unless a final definitive agreement relating to a Possible Transaction is entered into, the Potential Buyer shall not, by virtue of this Agreement, have any claims whatsoever against the other Company, the Representatives of the other Company or any of their respective directors, officers, stockholders, owners, affiliates or agents arising out of or relating to any Possible Transaction except with respect to explicit obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Evaluation Material is to be used 'for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction', while the changed text states it is to be used 'solely for marketing purposes'. This directly contradicts the fundamental purpose of the agreement as stated in the contradicted text, which specifies that 'no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists' until a definitive agreement is reached. If the material is used for marketing, it undermines the evaluation purpose and creates a conflict with the understanding that the agreement is not a transaction agreement, causing uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party as described by structural flaws.

perturbation.original_text: Each Recipient agrees that it and its Representatives will not to use any Evaluation Material of the Provider in violation of applicable securities laws.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Recipient agrees that it and its Representatives will not to use any Evaluation Material of the Provider, unless they believe it benefits them to violate applicable securities laws.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase ", unless they believe it benefits them to violate applicable securities laws." this creates a contradiction to what was originally intended in the agreement. It creates a conflict between the initial compliance statement and the discretionary opt-out. This especially affects section 14, where is states that money is not sufficient for breach of agreement. If that is the case, that means that securities laws do matter but this added sentence in text contradicts this
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Company understands and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Company or any of its Representatives and that the Company against which such breach is committed shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by either Company of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Company against which such breach is committed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the original agreement, allowing violation of securities laws under certain circumstances, which directly contradicts the contradicted text stating that money damages are not a sufficient remedy for any breach of the agreement. The contradicted text emphasizes equitable relief, indicating the importance of compliance, but the changed text undermines this by creating a loophole, creating a strong in-text contradiction based on the structural flaws definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall terminate five years after the date of this Agreement (except with respect to trade secrets for which the term shall be perpetual).
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall terminate five years after the date of this Agreement (except with respect to all information disclosed, the term shall be perpetual).
perturbation.explanation: By extending the perpetual protection to "all information disclosed" instead of just "trade secrets," this contradicts Section 2(a) which defines exceptions to Evaluation Material, such as information that becomes publicly available. This implies that even information that later becomes public is still perpetually protected, contradicting the explicit exceptions laid out in the definitions section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term “ Evaluation Material ” means information concerning the Provider which has been or is furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below) in connection with the Recipient’s evaluation of a Possible Transaction, including its business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities, and includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives which contain or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished by the Provider hereunder. The term Evaluation Material does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was within the Recipient’s possession prior to its being furnished to the Recipient by or on behalf of the Provider, provided that the source of such information was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, (iii) is or becomes available to the Recipient on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Provider or its Representatives, provided that such source is not bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information or (iv) is or has been independently developed by an employee, agent or contractor of the Recipient without use or reference to any information furnished by Provider or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text expands perpetual protection to 'all information disclosed', conflicting with the contradicted text's definition of 'Evaluation Material'. Specifically, the contradicted text states that information which becomes publicly available is *not* considered Evaluation Material. The changed text effectively nullifies this exception by perpetually protecting all disclosed information, even if it later becomes public, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as it alters a definition in a way that breaks consistency.

perturbation.original_text: Each Company understands and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Company or any of its Representatives and that the Company against which such breach is committed shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Company understands and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Company or any of its Representatives, unless the breaching party elects to pay money damages as full compensation. If the breaching party so elects, then only money damages shall be available.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the ability for a breaching party to elect for only money damages, creates a contradiction to the sentence prior that indicates that 'money damages would not be a sufficient remedy'. This creates an intext contradiction. This affects section 15, Legal Fees, since now it has to be factored in
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that either Company or its Representatives has breached this Agreement, then the Company which is, or the Company whose Representatives are, determined to have so breached shall be liable and pay to the other Company the reasonable legal fees and costs incurred by the other Company in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.justification: The original text states money damages are not a sufficient remedy, implying equitable relief is always available. The changed text introduces an option for the breaching party to elect money damages as full compensation, contradicting the initial assertion that money damages are insufficient. This directly impacts the contradicted text regarding legal fees, because Section 15 stipulates that the breaching party is liable for legal fees in the event of litigation relating to the agreement if they are determined to have breached the agreement. However, the change in text indicates that now, money damages may apply if the breaching party elects to pay for them, which is a remedy that is not in line with Section 15. This creates a strong contradiction as it changes the nature of remedies and potentially nullifies the basis for legal fee recovery under the original terms.

perturbation.original_text: No failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or future exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Any failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall be considered a permanent waiver thereof if not exercised within 30 days of the event giving rise to the right. Any single or partial exercise thereof shall preclude any other or future exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: Reversing the clause concerning the waiver of rights by introducing a 30-day limit and prohibiting subsequent exercises contradicts the original intent, now a delay will be considered a permanent waiver. This new rule also conflicts with section 13 that mentions: No provision of this Agreement can be waived or amended in favor of either Party except by written consent of the other Party, which consent shall specifically refer to such provision and explicitly make such waiver or amendment
perturbation.contradicted_text: No provision of this Agreement can be waived or amended in favor of either Party except by written consent of the other Party, which consent shall specifically refer to such provision and explicitly make such waiver or amendment. No failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or future exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a 30-day limit for exercising rights, which is considered a permanent waiver if not exercised within that time. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that no failure or delay shall operate as a waiver. It also contradicts the section stating that no provision can be waived except by written consent, as the changed text introduces an automatic waiver mechanism without written consent. Therefore, the change creates a clear and strong in-text contradiction.
